content: C enum values are not automatically prepended with the enum name, so C code makes a habit of manually prefixing enum values to ensure there are no collisions for the user. This is a common pattern for enums across the kernel. That means if bindgen prefixes with the enum name, we end up with a goofy/verbose double prefix. Not the end of the world, but a very annoying paper cut e.g. when working with uAPI headers.

For example, my wip branch of drm/asahi has the following code:

         match data.op {
            uapi::drm_asahi_bind_object_op_DRM_ASAHI_BIND_OBJECT_OP_BIND => {
                 Self::do_gem_bind_object(device, dev_data, data, file)
             }
            uapi::drm_asahi_bind_object_op_DRM_ASAHI_BIND_OBJECT_OP_UNBIND => {
                 Self::do_gem_unbind_object(device, dev_data, data, file)
             }

enum drm_asahi_bind_object_op {
        DRM_ASAHI_BIND_OBJECT_OP_BIND = 0,
        DRM_ASAHI_BIND_OBJECT_OP_UNBIND = 1,
};
It would be much nicer if we could set --no-prepend-enum-name when calling bindgen. This would let the code be a much more compact / close to the original C enum, uapi::DRM_ASAHI_BIND_OBJECT_OP_BIND

I think it makes sense to do this globally in R4L. But if we're going to, it makes sense to do so sooner than later because I imagine it'll require a cross-tree flag day change and that's a lot easier to do before we have a huge pile of Rust upstream.
+1 to this if possible. I also found these huge names a huge hassle.

For hrtimer I just did Rust versions of the enums:

/// Restart policy for timers.
#[derive(Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug)]
#[repr(u32)]
pub enum HrTimerRestart {
    /// Timer should not be restarted.
    NoRestart = bindings::hrtimer_restart_HRTIMER_NORESTART as u32,
    /// Timer should be restarted.
    Restart = bindings::hrtimer_restart_HRTIMER_RESTART as u32,
}

That allows better naming and attaching proper documentation to the variants. Depending on how many enums you are working with, this might be viable for you?

@Andreas Hindborg sounds like duplication, which can easily introduce bugs. Imagine how painful this would be to debug if NoRestart was hooked to HRTIMER_RESTART and vice versa.

Also, IMHO the docs for the uapi stuff should be picked up from the C docs themselves. Isn't that already the case, as in, done automatically by bindgen?

\

Daniel Almeida said:

@Andreas Hindborg sounds like duplication, which can easily introduce bugs. Imagine how painful this would be to debug if NoRestart was hooked to HRTIMER_RESTART and vice versa.

Not sure if there are some switches for bindgen, but at least the things I get by default is this:

pub const hrtimer_restart_HRTIMER_NORESTART: hrtimer_restart = 0;
pub const hrtimer_restart_HRTIMER_RESTART: hrtimer_restart = 1;
pub type hrtimer_restart = ffi::c_uint;

So the bindgen thing is not an enum, and we don't get any exhaustiveness checks.

As for the docs, the C repr for this hrtimer enum is:

/*
 * Return values for the callback function
 */
enum hrtimer_restart {
    HRTIMER_NORESTART,  /* Timer is not restarted */
    HRTIMER_RESTART,    /* Timer must be restarted */
};

I don't see those docs in the bindgen output.

See this example for how binder deals with it:
https://rust.docs.kernel.org/macros/macro.concat_idents.html

Pasting from discussion elsewhere:--rustified-enums actually generate the code I hand write, but it is UB to accept that type on the FFI interface, if the value passed is not one of the valid discriminant. Work to generate conversion functions automatically is in progress: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-bindgen/pull/2908

I tested this today, it seems constified_enum_module doesn't respect prepend_enum_name so @Daniel Almeida @Alyssa Rosenzweig you can just start passing --constified-enum-module to the enums you want to be put in a module without the prefix prepended.

cc @Miguel Ojeda

Andreas Hindborg said:

For hrtimer I just did Rust versions of the enums:

/// Restart policy for timers.
#[derive(Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug)]
#[repr(u32)]
pub enum HrTimerRestart {
    /// Timer should not be restarted.
    NoRestart = bindings::hrtimer_restart_HRTIMER_NORESTART as u32,
    /// Timer should be restarted.
    Restart = bindings::hrtimer_restart_HRTIMER_RESTART as u32,
}

That allows better naming and attaching proper documentation to the variants. Depending on how many enums you are working with, this might be viable for you?

Even if you do this, this snippet of Rust code would still be nicer with the prefixes cut, so it's not either/or IMO
IIUC, bindgen is kernel-wide so I don't know how I'd do that without coordinating with everyone
